html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 《續夷堅志》 - Title of the text, meaning 'Continuation of the *Yijian Zhi*.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋 - Refers to the Song Dynasty, a period of significant cultural and literary development in Chinese history.
  • 元好問 - The author of the text, a renowned poet, historian, and scholar of the Jin and early Yuan periods.
  • 《續夷堅志》 - A continuation of *Yijian Zhi*, a collection of strange tales and anecdotes compiled by Lu Dajun during the Song Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 《續夷堅志》 - Title of the text.
  • 宋 - The Song Dynasty.
  • 元好問 - The author of the text.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 國家將興，必有祯祥；國家將亡，必有妖孽 - Classical proverb meaning 'When a state is about to rise, there will be auspicious signs; when a state is about to fall, there will be ominous signs.'
  • 平世有闲耳 - Literally means 'in a peaceful era, it is just a matter of leisure,' implying that the content of *Economic and Political Miscellany* and *Continued Economic and Political Miscellany* is less significant in times of peace.
  • 砦窳叟 - A pseudonym or pen name, possibly indicating a humble or self-deprecating author.
  • 博学且勤 - Means 'diligent and studious,' describing Wáng Qǐshàn's character.
  • 异书 - Refers to unusual or rare books.
  • 手钞之 - Means 'to copy by hand,' emphasizing the personal effort involved.
  • 耳闻目见 - Means 'heard and seen with one's own ears and eyes,' indicating firsthand knowledge.
  • 劝善戒恶 - Means 'to encourage good deeds and warn against evil,' a common theme in moral literature.
  • 推广之心 - Means 'the heart of spreading or extending,' suggesting a desire to disseminate knowledge.
  • 陆沈 - A metaphor for the fall of a country or the decline of a civilization.
  • 枣本 - Refers to a manuscript or edition bound with a cover made from a jujube (枣) tree.
  • 花朝日 - Refers to the Festival of Flowers, a traditional Chinese festival celebrating the arrival of spring.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 子思子 - Refers to Zǐsī (子思), a Confucian philosopher and grandson of Confucius, known for his work *The Book of Filial Piety*.
  • 洪景卢 - Refers to Hóng Jǐnglú (洪景卢), a scholar and compiler of *Economic and Political Miscellany* (*夷坚志*), a collection of stories and anecdotes from the Song dynasty.
  • 元好问 - Refers to Yuán Hǎowèn (元好问), a famous poet and historian of the Jin dynasty, known for his work *Continued Economic and Political Miscellany* (*续志*).
  • 政、宣 - Refers to the reigns of Emperor Huizong (政和) and Emperor Ningzong (宣和) of the Song dynasty.
  • 泰和、贞祐 - Refers to the reigns of Emperor Xuanzong (泰和) and Emperor Xianzong (贞祐) of the Jin dynasty.
  • 遗山先生 - Refers to Yuán Hǎowèn, who is also known as '遗山先生' (Mr. Yishan).
  • 陆沈 - A metaphor for the fall of a country or the decline of a civilization, often used to describe the Jin dynasty's decline.
  • 至顺三年 - Refers to the third year of the Zhisun era (1332 AD) during the Yuan dynasty.
  • 至正二十三年癸卯岁 - Refers to the twenty-third year of the Zhizheng era (1363 AD) during the Yuan dynasty, the year of the Water Rabbit.
  • 泗北村居映雪斋 - Refers to a place named '映雪斋' (Snow Reflection Study) in the village of Sibei (泗北).
  • 华亭 - Refers to Huating, a place in present-day Shanghai, known for its cultural significance.
  • 在家道人孙道明 - Refers to Sūn Dàomíng (孙道明), a Buddhist monk or scholar.
  • 北地枣本 - Refers to a manuscript or edition bound with a cover made from a jujube tree, originating from the north.
  • 武林新刻《金史》 - Refers to a new edition of the *History of the Jin Dynasty* (*金史*) published in Wulin (present-day Hangzhou).

Proper Nouns

  • 子思子 - Zǐsī (子思), a Confucian philosopher.
  • 洪景卢 - Hóng Jǐnglú (洪景卢), a scholar and compiler of *Economic and Political Miscellany*.
  • 元好问 - Yuán Hǎowèn (元好问), a famous poet and historian of the Jin dynasty.
  • 遗山先生 - Yuán Hǎowèn, also known as '遗山先生.'
  • 朱方石岩民瞻氏 - The name of the person who wrote the preface, dated to 1332 AD.
  • 吴中王起善 - Wáng Qǐshàn (王起善), a scholar from Wu region.
  • 芥甫夏侯 - The current owner of the manuscript.
  • 孙道明 - Sūn Dàomíng (孙道明), a Buddhist monk or scholar.
  • 北地枣本 - A manuscript or edition bound with a jujube tree cover, originating from the north.
  • 武林 - Wulin, present-day Hangzhou.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遺山先生 - Refers to Yuwen Xuzhong, a famous poet and scholar of the Jin Dynasty, known for his contributions to literature and history.
  • 浮湛散秩 - Describes a state of floating and sinking in low-ranking positions, indicating a life of fluctuating fortunes.
  • 洊登左司 - Means 'repeatedly appointed to the Left Censorate,' a high-ranking official position.
  • 旋遭喪亂 - Means 'soon after suffering from chaos and disaster,' referring to the turbulent times following the fall of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 寄意于《中州》一集 - Means 'expressing his thoughts in the *Zhongzhou* anthology,' indicating his literary efforts during his later years.
  • 吊詭之厄言 - Refers to 'strange and ominous words,' suggesting the nature of the content in *Yiji Zhi*.
  • 禨祥之外乘 - Means 'a vehicle for auspicious and ominous signs,' implying that the text contains elements of superstition and omens.
  • 不賢識小 - A self-deprecating phrase meaning 'not being a great man, I recognize small things,' indicating humility and a focus on minor details.
  • 手钞付梓 - Means 'hand-copying and printing,' describing the process of publishing the text.
  • 依翁氏所辑遺山《年譜》 - Means 'following Wang Zhen's compilation of Yuwen Xuzhong's *Chronological Biography*,' indicating the use of a historical reference for context.
  • 庶覽者粗悉其平生云 - Means 'so that readers may roughly understand his life,' expressing the translator's intention to provide a basic understanding of Yuwen Xuzhong's life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金源末造 - Refers to the late period of the Jin Dynasty, characterized by political instability and decline.
  • 備位下僚 - Describes holding a low-ranking position, often used to express dissatisfaction with one's status.
  • 左司 - Refers to the Left Censorate, a high-ranking administrative position in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 中州 - Refers to the central plains of China, often used to denote the heartland of Chinese civilization.
  • 夷堅志 - A collection of stories and anecdotes compiled by Lin Yutang, focusing on strange and supernatural events.
  • 洪氏 - Refers to Hong Mai, a famous scholar and compiler of *Yiji Zhi*.
  • 百六之會 - A term from Chinese astrology, referring to a period of great change and upheaval.
  • 五行乖舛 - Means 'the five elements are out of balance,' indicating a time of natural and social disorder.
  • 開必先 - Means 'the beginning of a new era,' suggesting a transition period.
  • 東京琐事 - Refers to trivial matters from the Eastern Capital (Luoyang), indicating the focus on everyday life.
  • 大梁書院 - Refers to the Great Liang Academy in Kaifeng, Henan Province, a significant educational institution.
  • 嘉慶戊辰冬日 - Refers to the winter of the year 1808 during the reign of Emperor Jiaqing.
  • 杭郡余集 - Refers to Yu Ji, a scholar and translator from Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province.
  • 讀易樓 - Refers to the *Read Yi* Tower, a place where classical texts were studied.
  • 筠圃先生 - Refers to Yuwen Xuzhong's father, Yuwen Junpu, a respected scholar.

Proper Nouns

  • 遺山先生 - Yuwen Xuzhong
  • 金源 - Jin Dynasty
  • 左司 - Left Censorate
  • 中州 - Central Plains
  • 夷堅志 - *Yiji Zhi*
  • 洪氏 - Hong Mai
  • 百六之會 - A period of great change
  • 五行 - Five elements
  • 東京 - Eastern Capital (Luoyang)
  • 大梁書院 - Great Liang Academy
  • 嘉慶 - Reign of Emperor Jiaqing
  • 杭郡余集 - Yu Ji
  • 讀易樓 - *Read Yi* Tower
  • 筠圃先生 - Yuwen Junpu

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鎮庫寶 - Literally 'treasure of the treasury,' a term used to describe valuable items stored in a state treasury. In this context, it refers to the alchemical pill.
  • 煉丹成 - Refers to the successful completion of the alchemical process of refining an elixir of immortality.
  • 不及餌 - Means 'not yet taken as a remedy' or 'not yet consumed.'
  • 藏之鎮州庫藏中者余三百年 - Literally 'stored in the treasury of Zhenzhou for over three hundred years.' This phrase indicates the long period during which the pill was kept in the treasury.
  • 真定元帥三喜棄城 - Refers to the act of the military commander of Zhending (真定) named Sanxi abandoning the city.
  • 取之以行 - Means 'took it with him when he left.'
  • 行及平陽 - Means 'when he reached Pingyang.'
  • 為胥莘公所劾 - Means 'was impeached by Xushen Gong,' indicating that the pill was seized due to legal action.
  • 收之 - Means 'seized it' or 'took it.'
  • 丹入汴京，下豐衍庫收 - Means 'the pill was brought to Kaifeng and stored in the Fengyan Treasury.'
  • 京城變後 - Refers to the chaos or upheaval in the capital city after the event.
  • 予同戶部主事劉彥卿往觀之 - Means 'I went with Liu Yanei, the Director of the Ministry of Revenue, to see it.'
  • 丹以漆櫃盛 - Means 'the pill was stored in a lacquer cabinet.'
  • 旁畫廣成子問道像 - Means 'on the side of the cabinet, there was a painting of Guangchengzi asking about the Dao.'
  • 中復有漆合 - Means 'inside, there was another lacquer box.'
  • 高五寸，闊三寸 - Means 'five inches high and three inches wide.'
  • 合蓋上作九環 - Means 'there were nine rings on the lid.'
  • 外八中一 - Means 'eight on the outside and one in the middle.'
  • 以金塗之 - Means 'coated with gold.'
  • 各有流去聲道相貫 - Means 'each had a channel for the sound to flow through.'
  • 環中作小孔 - Means 'there were small holes in the rings.'
  • 予意其為九轉也 - Means 'I thought it was the result of nine transformations.'
  • 合中復有銀合盛丹 - Means 'inside the lacquer box, there was another silver box containing the pill.'
  • 合蓋上鏤佛一 - Means 'on the lid of the silver box, there was an engraved Buddha.'
  • 左龍右鳳 - Means 'a dragon on the left and a phoenix on the right.'
  • 在佛座下 - Means 'at the feet of the Buddha.'
  • 開視，丹體殊輕 - Means 'when opened, the pill was unusually light.'
  • 周匝合中 - Means 'all around the box.'
  • 色如棗皮漆 - Means 'the color was like the bark of a jujube tree.'
  • 而裂璺縱橫 - Means 'with cracks running in all directions.'
  • 絕不與今世丹砂相似 - Means 'completely different from the cinnabar of today.'
  • 予意頗輕之 - Means 'I thought little of it.'
  • 問主庫者此有何異 - Means 'I asked the person in charge of the treasury what was special about it.'
  • 無他，但陰晦中恆出光怪，如火起然耳 - Means 'nothing special, just that it constantly emitted strange light in dim conditions, like a fire.'
  • 壬辰年親見 - Means 'I personally saw it in the year Renchen.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 趙王鎔 - Refers to Zhao Wang Rong, a historical figure from the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 鎮州 - Refers to the city of Zhenzhou, an important location in ancient China.
  • 真定 - Refers to the city of Zhending, another important location in ancient China.
  • 胥莘公 - Refers to Xushen Gong, a historical figure who played a role in the events described.
  • 汴京 - Refers to the city of Kaifeng, the capital of the Song Dynasty.
  • 豐衍庫 - Refers to the Fengyan Treasury, a state treasury in Kaifeng.
  • 廣成子 - Refers to Guangchengzi, a legendary figure associated with Daoism and alchemy.
  • 九轉 - Refers to the concept of 'nine transformations,' a term used in alchemy to describe the process of refining an elixir.
  • 丹砂 - Refers to cinnabar, a mineral used in alchemy and medicine.

Proper Nouns

  • 趙王鎔 - Zhao Wang Rong
  • 鎮州 - Zhenzhou
  • 真定 - Zhending
  • 胥莘公 - Xushen Gong
  • 汴京 - Kaifeng
  • 豐衍庫 - Fengyan Treasury
  • 廣成子 - Guangchengzi
  • 九轉 - Nine Transformations
  • 丹砂 - Cinnabar

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 殆秉异气所化 - Suggests the stone was formed by unusual or supernatural forces.
  • 宜入新年，长命富贵 - Common New Year wishes for a prosperous and long life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 正大 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Jin.
  • 舞阳 - A county in Henan Province.
  • 河中 - Refers to the region around modern-day Yuncheng, Shanxi.
  • 城隍 - The guardian deity of a city, often associated with local protection and justice.
  • 桃符 - Peach wood charms used for protection and good fortune during the New Year.

Proper Nouns

  • 金狮猛 - The title of the story.
  • 张圣俞 - A historical figure.
  • 舞阳 - A county in Henan Province.
  • 河中 - A region in Shanxi Province.
  • 康伯禄 - A historical figure.
  • 李钦叔 - A historical figure.
  • 杨正卿 - The governor of Shan County.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 包女得嫁 - Concise phrase meaning 'the girl of the Bao family gets married.'
  • 世俗傳 - Means 'it is said in the world' or 'according to popular belief.'
  • 以正直主東岳速報司 - Complex phrase meaning 'to serve as the righteous and swift retribution officer of Mount Dong (the Eastern Peak).'
  • 山野小民 - Refers to 'common people in the mountains and countryside.'
  • 倡家 - Refers to 'courtesans' or 'prostitutes.'
  • 捶楚備至 - Means 'to be beaten severely' or 'to suffer harsh punishment.'
  • 里中一女巫 - Means 'a village woman who practices witchcraft.'
  • 私謂人云 - Means 'privately told someone that.'
  • 閉目吁氣 - Means 'to close one's eyes and exhale deeply,' often associated with entering a trance.
  • 屈伸良久 - Means 'to bend and stretch for a long time,' describing a ritualistic movement.
  • 作神降之態 - Means 'to act as if a god has descended.'
  • 少之 - Means 'after a while' or 'in a short time.'
  • 瞑目咄咤 - Means 'to close one's eyes and shout angrily.'
  • 呼主人者出 - Means 'to call the owner out.'
  • 大罵之 - Means 'to curse them severely.'
  • 具香火 - Means 'to prepare incense and fire,' indicating ritual preparation.
  • 俯伏請罪 - Means 'to prostrate oneself and ask for forgiveness.'
  • 何所触尊神 - Means 'what have you offended the great god?'
  • 速報司 - Refers to 'the swift retribution officer.'
  • 汝何敢以我孙女为倡 - Means 'how dare you make my granddaughter a prostitute?'
  • 限汝十日 - Means 'I give you ten days.'
  • 不嫁之良家 - Means 'if you do not marry her to a good family.'
  • 吾灭汝门矣 - Means 'I will destroy your family!'
  • 百拜谢过 - Means 'to bow a hundred times to apologize.'
  • 不数日嫁之 - Means 'within a few days, she was married.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 包希文 - Refers to 'Bao Xiwen,' a historical figure believed to be a righteous and swift retribution officer of Mount Dong (the Eastern Peak).
  • 东岳 - Refers to 'Mount Dong,' one of the Five Great Mountains in China, often associated with the god of mountains and the underworld.
  • 速报司 - Refers to 'the swift retribution officer,' a supernatural figure in Chinese folklore responsible for swift justice and retribution.
  • 倡家 - Refers to 'courtesans' or 'prostitutes,' a social class in ancient China.
  • 里中一女巫 - Refers to 'a village woman who practices witchcraft,' a common figure in rural Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 包希文 - The name of a historical figure believed to be a righteous and swift retribution officer of Mount Dong.
  • 东岳 - The name of Mount Dong, one of the Five Great Mountains in China.
  • 速报司 - The name of a supernatural figure in Chinese folklore responsible for swift justice and retribution.
  • 倡家 - The name of a social class in ancient China, referring to courtesans or prostitutes.
  • 里中一女巫 - The name of a village woman who practices witchcraft.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鐵中蟲 - Literal meaning 'insect in iron,' referring to a small creature found inside a cooking vessel; possibly symbolizes something hidden or unexpected.
  • 釜鳴不祥 - Superstitious belief that the sound of a cauldron boiling is inauspicious.
  • 廢不敢用 - Abandoned and not dared to use due to superstition.
  • 妨大眾作食 - Hinders the making of food for the community.
  • 椎破釜底 - Pounded the bottom of the cauldron.
  • 穴中得一蟲 - Found a creature in the hole.
  • 特執方之論耳 - Merely a strict doctrine, possibly unfounded belief.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吾州會長老 - The abbot of our temple, head of a Buddhist monastery.
  • 飛狐之團崖 - The mountain cliff of Feixu, a specific location in the region.
  • 典座僧 - The monk in charge of the kitchen, responsible for food preparation.
  • 廚堂一镬 - A large cooking pot in the kitchen, used for communal meals.
  • 魏文帝《典論》 - Emperor Wei Wen's *Dian Lun*, a historical text discussing literature and governance.
  • 全唯識記 - Entirely based on the theory of consciousness-only, a philosophical school in Buddhism.

Proper Nouns

  • 魏文帝 - Emperor Wei Wen (Cao Pi).
  • 典論 - *Dian Lun*, a historical text.
  • 飛狐 - Feixu, a place name.
  • 團崖 - The mountain cliff, a place name.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外力 - Term that can be translated as 'external force' or 'external strength'; in context, refers to a nickname or title for Wang Zengshou, emphasizing his physical strength or prowess.
  • 善角抵 - Proficient in jiao di, an ancient form of wrestling or grappling.
  • 钉去声驼足令跛 - Compound phrase meaning 'nail the camel's foot to make it lame'; '钉去声' may be a phonetic annotation indicating that the verb '钉' (to nail) is pronounced with a falling tone.
  • 自羊头村背负驼至代州 - Literal description of an action: 'From Yangtou Village, he carried the camel to Dazhou'; context suggests it is a test or demonstration.
  • 州守信以为然 - 'The state governor believed it to be true'; reflects the governor’s trust in the demonstration.
  • 石中蛇蝎 - 'Snakes and scorpions in the stone'; a metaphor or supernatural phenomenon.
  • 怨毒所化 - 'Transformed from hatred and poison'; implies a supernatural or spiritual origin.
  • 随想而入 - 'Enter according to one’s thoughts'; suggests that the creatures entered due to the person's thoughts or emotions.
  • 历千万劫而不得解者 - 'Those who have endured countless lifetimes without being resolved'; a Buddhist or Taoist expression referring to karmic entanglements or unresolved suffering.
  • 若不为解却 - 'If not resolved,' or 'If not dispelled'; a conditional clause.
  • 他日亦道曾见我来 - 'One day, they will say they have seen me come'; a predictive or prophetic statement.
  • 全唯识说 - 'Fully in line with the theory of consciousness-only (Yi Shi)'; a reference to a Buddhist philosophical system.
  • 任氏翁媪 - 'The elderly couple of the Ren family'; '翁媪' refers to an old man and an old woman.
  • 同年月日时生 - 'Born on the same day, month, year, and hour'; a rare and significant event in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 同年月日时死 - 'Died on the same day, month, year, and hour'; an even rarer event and is considered a unique occurrence in history.
  • 郑叟犯土禁 - 'Old man Zheng violated the earth taboo'; '叟' means old man, and '犯土禁' refers to violating a prohibition or taboo related to the earth or land.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泰和 - The 'Taihe' era was a reign period of the Jin Dynasty, specifically under Emperor Wanyan Liang (1220–1234).
  • 角抵 - An ancient Chinese form of wrestling or sumo-like combat. It was often performed in public and was considered a test of strength.
  • 代州 - Dazhou was a historical state or region in northern China, now part of Shanxi Province.
  • 樊帅 - Fang Shuai is likely a historical or literary figure, possibly a general or commander from the Jin or Yuan period.
  • 石中蛇蝎 - This phrase may refer to a supernatural or feng shui-related belief, where a stone contains hidden dangers (snakes and scorpions) that can affect a person’s fate or well-being.
  • 唯识说 - A branch of Mahayana Buddhism that emphasizes the theory of consciousness-only (vijnaptimatrata). This indicates the text may be influenced by Buddhist philosophy.
  • 任氏翁媪 - The story of the elderly couple from the Ren family is likely a local legend or anecdote, emphasizing the uniqueness of their synchronized birth and death.
  • 郑叟犯土禁 - This phrase may refer to a local taboo or superstition regarding land or earth. In traditional Chinese culture, certain rituals or prohibitions were associated with the earth, especially in farming or construction.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 资性强 - Means 'having a strong character' or 'being of a robust disposition.'
  • 不信禁忌 - Means 'not believing in taboos' or 'disregarding prohibitions.'
  • 太岁所在 - Refers to the position of the *Tai Sui* (太岁), a term in Chinese astrology that denotes a specific direction or location each year, which is considered inauspicious to disturb.
  • 我即太岁，尚何忌耶 - A defiant statement meaning 'I am the *Tai Sui*, what should I fear?'
  • 督役夫兴作 - Means 'supervising the laborers to begin construction.'
  • 掘地不二尺 - Means 'digging the ground to a depth of not two feet.'
  • 得妇人红绣鞋一双 - Means 'found a pair of red embroidered shoes belonging to a woman.'
  • 役夫欲罢作 - Means 'the laborers wanted to stop the work.'
  • 取焚之 - Means 'took them and burned them.'
  • 掘地愈急 - Means 'dug the ground more urgently.'
  • 得一黑鱼 - Means 'found a black fish.'
  • 烹食之 - Means 'cooked and ate it.'
  • 不旬日 - Means 'within ten days.'
  • 翁母并亡 - Means 'the old man's mother also died.'
  • 又丧长子 - Means 'and lost his eldest son.'
  • 连延十余口 - Means 'affecting more than ten people.'
  • 马十，牛四十 - Means 'ten horses and forty cattle.'
  • 死病狼藉 - Means 'deaths and illnesses were scattered.'
  • 存者大惧，避他所 - Means 'the survivors were greatly afraid and fled to another place.'
  • 祸乃息 - Means 'the disaster then ceased.'
  • 以捕鹑为业 - Means 'making a living by catching quails.'
  • 故人目为鹌鹑 - Means 'people therefore called him Quail.'
  • 止一儿，成童矣 - Means 'he had only one son, who had grown into a young man.'
  • 一旦死 - Means 'one day he died.'
  • 翁媪自念老无所倚 - Means 'the old man and his wife thought of their own old age with no one to rely on.'
  • 号哭闷绝，恨不俱死 - Means 'they wept and fainted, hating that they could not die together.'
  • 明日欲埋之，又复不忍 - Means 'the next day they wanted to bury him, but could not bear to do so.'
  • 但累砖作邱 - Means 'they simply piled up bricks to make a mound.'
  • 吾儿还活 - Means 'our son will come back to life.'
  • 撒砖曳棺出 - Means 'they removed the bricks and dragged the coffin out.'
  • 舁归 - Means 'carried him back home.'
  • 儿果复活 - Means 'the son indeed came back to life.'
  • 俄索汤粥 - Means 'soon he asked for soup and porridge.'
  • 良久，说初为人摄往冥司 - Means 'after a while, he said he was first taken by someone to the underworld.'
  • 儿哀诉主者 - Means 'the son pleaded with the officials in tears.'
  • 爹娘老可念，乞尽余年，葬送毕，死无所归恨 - Means 'my parents are old and pitiable, I beg to live out my remaining years, to finish the funeral rites, and then die without regret.'
  • 冥官颇怜之 - Means 'the officials of the underworld pitied him.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '马三出契券为质，竟夺之' is somewhat elliptical. '出契券为质' (chū qì quàn wéi zhì) means 'to produce a contract as proof,' and '竟夺之' (jìng duó zhī) means 'and ultimately took it.'
  • The sentence '袁春家犬乳数子，中一小花狗，腹毛纯白，有朱书' describes a dog with a red inscription on its belly. '乳数子' (rǔ shù zǐ) means 'to nurse several puppies,' and '腹毛纯白' (fù máo chún bái) means 'with pure white belly fur.'
  • The sentence '马三素多怨家，竟欲出钱买之' means 'Ma San had many enemies and eventually wanted to buy it.' '素多怨家' (sù duō yuàn jiā) means 'had many enemies,' and '竟欲出钱买之' (jìng yù chū qián mǎi zhī) means 'eventually wanted to buy it.'
  • The sentence '问所以能怪变者，皆托以天神所为' means 'asked how he could create strange phenomena, and all claimed it was done by gods.' '问所以能怪变者' (wèn suǒ yǐ néng guài biàn zhě) means 'asked how he could create strange phenomena,' and '皆托以天神所为' (jiē tuō yǐ tiān shén suǒ wéi) means 'all claimed it was done by gods.'
  • The sentence '决杖二百而死' means 'to be beaten with a stick two hundred times and die.' '决杖' (jué zhàng) means 'to be beaten with a stick,' and '二百' (èr bǎi) means 'two hundred times.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又一时辰许 - A phrase indicating a period of time, roughly 'another hour or so.' The word *许* is a particle used to express approximation.
  • 白面炒熟 - 'White flour fried until cooked' – likely referring to a medicinal preparation, where flour is cooked as part of a process.
  • 蜜蜡为丸 - 'made into pills with honey wax' – a traditional method of making medicinal pills.
  • 如桐子大 - 'as large as a paulownia seed' – *桐子* is a type of seed used as a standard for size in classical texts.
  • 温白汤或乳香汤下百丸 - 'taken with warm white soup or frankincense soup, one hundred pills at a time' – a standard medicinal dosage.
  • 名曰“后药” - 'named 'the later medicine'' – *后药* is a term used to distinguish this from an earlier phase of treatment.
  • 先已饱食 - 'having already eaten a full meal' – indicates the timing of the treatment relative to food intake.
  • 故二三日不困 - 'thus, for two or three days, one does not feel tired' – *困* here likely refers to fatigue or weakness.
  • 虽困亦无伤 - 'even if tired, it is harmless' – a reassurance that the treatment is safe.
  • 服药后当万缘不染 - 'after taking the medicine, one should remain unaffected by worldly matters' – *万缘不染* is a Buddhist term meaning to remain unattached or unaffected by external distractions.
  • 夫心动则气散，语多则气伤 - 'When the mind is agitated, qi disperses; when one speaks too much, qi is harmed' – classical expressions linking mental and physical well-being.
  • 辟谷者以宁心养气为本 - 'those practicing辟谷 (fasting) should take calming the mind and nurturing qi as the foundation' – *辟谷* is an ancient practice of fasting or abstaining from food to cultivate internal energy.
  • 事来则应，事过勿留于心 - 'respond to events as they come, but do not dwell on them afterward' – a key principle in Daoist and Buddhist practices of non-attachment.
  • 时时向日咽气 - 'frequently swallow air while facing the sun' – a practice to absorb solar energy and replenish qi.
  • 茶汤任意 - 'tea and soup may be consumed freely' – indicates that some liquids are permitted.
  • 勿食有滓之物 - 'do not eat things with residue' – likely referring to foods that are difficult to digest or leave residue in the body.
  • 忌怒，忌大劳 - 'avoid anger, avoid excessive labor' – warnings to maintain internal balance.
  • 十日后，饥肉虽瘦，而筋骨轻健，神观开朗 - 'after ten days, although the body may be lean, the tendons and bones become light and strong, and the spirit becomes clear and open' – describes the physical and mental effects of the practice.
  • 如欲开食，须二七日以后 - 'if one wishes to resume eating, it must be after fourteen days' – *二七日* is a numerical expression for fourteen days.
  • 候药在丹田 - 'wait until the medicine has reached the dan tian' – *丹田* is a key concept in Chinese internal alchemy, referring to the lower abdomen as the center of qi.
  • 不及二七日而食，则药随脏腑而下矣 - 'if one eats before fourteen days, the medicine will descend with the internal organs' – suggesting that premature eating may disrupt the process.
  • 开食之后，如更欲住食，不必服药，止以乳香汤匀之 - 'after resuming eating, if one wishes to stop again, it is not necessary to take medicine, only a frankincense soup is needed to balance it' – indicates that the process can be paused or resumed with simpler means.
  • 凶年饥岁，至父子夫妇相啖 - 'in years of famine, even fathers and sons or husbands and wives eat each other' – a grim description of extreme food scarcity.
  • 捣为泥丸，作弹子大 - 'pounded into a paste, made into a pill the size of a bullet' – a description of a medicinal preparation.
  • 黄丹为衣 - 'with yellow cinnabar as a coating' – *黄丹* is a type of cinnabar or vermilion used in traditional medicine.
  • 纸带子盛此药一丸，缝合著脐中 - 'a paper strip is used to hold one pill, sewn onto the navel' – a method of applying the medicine externally.
  • 上用裹肚系定 - 'fastened with a wrap-around garment' – likely a band or cloth used to secure the medicine.
  • 每遇箭镞未出，先如上系定 - 'each time an arrowhead has not yet been expelled, first apply it as described above' – a method for treating arrow wounds.
  • 顷用象牙末擦疮口 - 'then use powdered ivory to rub on the wound' – a traditional remedy.
  • 若中箭已久，须用锋刃或针，少少取破，搽象牙末，则箭镞自出 - 'if the arrow has been in for a long time, use a sharp blade or needle to slightly puncture, then apply ivory powder, and the arrowhead will come out by itself' – a detailed procedure for removing an embedded arrow.
  • 如鱼骨鲠喉，以至针、钱、麦芒，不限久近，皆验 - 'whether it is a fish bone stuck in the throat, a needle, a coin, or a wheat awn, regardless of how long it has been there, this method is effective' – a claim about the versatility of the treatment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 辟谷 - A Daoist practice of fasting or abstaining from food to cultivate internal energy (*qi*). It is often associated with alchemical and spiritual purification.
  • 丹田 - A central concept in Daoist and Chinese martial arts, referring to the lower abdomen as the center of energy cultivation.
  • 乳香汤 - A traditional medicinal soup made with frankincense (*乳香*), used in both healing and spiritual practices.
  • 黄丹 - Yellow cinnabar, a red mineral often used in traditional Chinese medicine and alchemy, believed to have energetic and medicinal properties.
  • 纸带子 - A strip of paper used in traditional medicine to hold or apply pills, often for external application.
  • 裹肚 - A garment or wrap used to secure medicinal applications, especially around the navel.
  • 象牙末 - Powdered ivory, used in traditional remedies for wounds and foreign bodies.
  • 箭镞 - Arrowheads, which were common in ancient warfare and often required medical treatment if embedded in the body.
  • 鱼骨鲠喉 - A fish bone stuck in the throat, a common medical issue in ancient times.
  • 针、钱、麦芒 - Needle, coin, and wheat awn – all examples of foreign objects that could become lodged in the body, particularly the throat.
  • 凶年饥岁 - A term for years of famine or extreme food scarcity, often leading to desperate measures like cannibalism.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雷氏節姑 - Title translating to 'The Virtuous Maiden of the Lei Family.' '節' (jié) means 'virtue' or 'integrity,' and '姑' (gū) means 'maiden' or 'girl.'
  • 西仲、南仲從姊妹行 - Indicates that the Lei family members are related to the Western and Southern branches of the family. '從姊妹行' (cóng shì mèi xíng) means 'sisters-in-law' or 'cousins.'
  • 年十七，嫁為應州丁倅妻 - Translates to 'At seventeen, she married the deputy magistrate of Yingzhou.' '倅' (cuì) means 'deputy' or 'assistant.'
  • 雷氏群從有不悅者，讦告服内成亲 - Translates to 'Some members of the Lei family who were displeased, accused her of having a pre-marital relationship.' '讦告' (jié gào) means 'to accuse openly.'
  • 婚遂聽離 - Translates to 'The marriage was then annulled.' '聽離' (tīng lí) means 'to allow separation.'
  • 再適人否 - Translates to 'Will you remarry?' '適' (shì) means 'to marry' or 'to suit.'
  • 我若再嫁，当令两目瞎 - Translates to 'If I remarry, I will make my eyes blind.' '两目瞎' (liǎng mù xiā) means 'blind in both eyes.'
  • 从孙希颜 - Translates to 'Her grandson, Xiyan.'
  • 青词之谴 - Translates to 'The punishment for the green script.' '青词' (qīng cí) refers to a type of prayer or petition written on green paper, often used in religious rituals.
  • 正和初 - Translates to 'In the early years of the Zhenghe era,' referring to the reign period of Emperor Huizong of Song (1101-1110).
  • 中牟阳桥人刘慎，字荣辅 - Translates to 'Liu Shen, a native of Yangqiao in Zhongmu, with the courtesy name Rongfu.'
  • 为里人刘六作延荐青词 - Translates to 'Wrote a green script to recommend Liu Liu to the gods.'
  • 刘平生有秽行 - Translates to 'Liu had a history of immoral behavior.'
  • 荣辅作词，曲为辨理 - Translates to 'Rongfu wrote the script, defending Liu's actions.'
  • 依例荐拔 - Translates to 'Following the usual procedure for recommendation.'
  • 数日，荣辅作首 - Translates to 'Several days later, Rongfu became the head.'
  • 学生鲁罗儿者，病中忽为物所凭 - Translates to 'A student named Lu Luo'er, who was suddenly possessed by a spirit while ill.'
  • 汝昨为刘六作青词，汝于谁处敢妄语耶 - Translates to 'You wrote a green script for Liu Liu yesterday. Who gave you the right to speak falsely?'
  • 惶惧殊甚 - Translates to 'He was extremely frightened.'
  • 手写首状 - Translates to 'Wrote a confession with his own hands.'
  • 言自后更不敢复作青词 - Translates to 'Stated that he would never write another green script again.'
  • 此儿甫成童 - Translates to 'This child was just a young boy.'
  • 初不知青词为何物 - Translates to 'Initially did not know what a green script was.'

  • 学书，下笔即有成人之风 - '学书' means 'to learn calligraphy,' and '下笔即有成人之风' means 'her writing already shows the style of an adult,' highlighting her advanced skill in calligraphy.
  • 旦夕家居，见家人或不整肃，以礼责之 - '旦夕家居' means 'at home every day,' and '见家人或不整肃，以礼责之' means 'if she sees family members who are not properly dressed or behave improperly, she rebukes them with courtesy,' showing her strict adherence to etiquette.
  • 又所诵书多能通大义，时为讲说 - '通大义' means 'understand the main ideas,' and '时为讲说' means 'sometimes she explains them,' indicating her ability to grasp and convey the deeper meanings of the texts.
  • 对属、才思敏捷 - '对属' means 'to match or correspond,' often used in the context of composing poetry, and '才思敏捷' means 'quick-witted and talented,' describing her intellectual agility.
  • 睡思昏昏如醉思，闺心寂寂似禅心 - A poetic expression where '睡思昏昏如醉思' means 'her drowsy thoughts are like those of someone drunk,' and '闺心寂寂似禅心' means 'her quiet heart is like that of a monk,' describing her state of mind.
  • 识者谓此诗不佳 - '识者' means 'those who understand' or 'scholars,' and '谓此诗不佳' means 'consider this poem to be poor,' indicating a critical assessment of her poetry.
  • 后日果得病，又四日亡 - '后日' means 'later on,' and '果得病，又四日亡' means 'she indeed fell ill and died four days later,' indicating the tragic end of her life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 永安钱 - A coin with the inscription 'Yong’an one-thousand.' The term 'Yong’an' may refer to a dynasty, place, or era. In Chinese history, the term '永安' appears in the reign titles of the Western Wei (535–556), but it is unclear if this is the same. The coin may be a rare or ancient denomination.
  • 海陵天德初 - Refers to the early years of the Tiande era under the reign of the Jurchen ruler Wanyan Liang (Emperor Xizong of the Jin), who ruled from 1149 to 1150. '海陵' is the name of Wanyan Liang, also known as Emperor Xizong of the Jin.
  • 燕 - Refers to the ancient state of Yan, later a region in northern China, often associated with the Jurchen state of Jin.
  • 析津府 - The name of a former administrative region in northern China, now part of Beijing. It was renamed 大兴 (Daxing), which later became the name of a district in modern Beijing.
  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China, often used as a model for naming cities or regions.
  • 汝阳 - A place name that may refer to a county or city in Henan or elsewhere. It was likely renamed from a previous name, possibly 永安.
  • 芝田 - Another place name, possibly in Henan, which was renamed from 永安.
  • 长宁 - A ward or district name, likely in the Jin capital, which was renamed from 永安.
  • 续夷坚志四 - A section of the *Xu Yi Jian Zhi* (*Continuation of the Yi Jian Zhi*), a classical Chinese collection of strange tales and historical notes. The *Yi Jian Zhi* was compiled by Zhang Zhihe during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 临淄 - A place name, referring to Linzhi, a city in Shandong Province.
  • 道院 - A Daoist temple or hall, indicating a religious or spiritual site.
  • 大定初 - Refers to the early years of the Dading era, the reign of Emperor Shizong of the Jin (1161–1189).
  • 山东人杨善渊 - A person from Shandong, indicating the geographical origin of the individual.
  • 大齐 - Refers to the Da Qi (Great Qi) dynasty, which is likely a misattribution or confusion with the Northern Qi (550–577), a dynasty of the Northern and Southern Dynasties period.
  • 天齐观 - A Daoist temple, likely referring to the Tianqi Temple, a well-known Daoist site in ancient China.
  • 玉清宫殿 - A palace or hall within the temple, often associated with the Yüqing Palace in Daoist temple architecture.
  • 天庆观 - A Daoist temple, likely referring to the Tianqing Temple, which was a temple of the Northern Qi dynasty.
  • 石羊 - A stone sheep, a common religious or symbolic object in ancient Chinese temples, often used as a guardian or decorative figure.
  • 高纬 - Gao Wei, the emperor of the Northern Qi dynasty (560–577), known for his eccentric behavior and reign.
  • 天统二年丙戌岁 - A specific year in the reign of Gao Wei, using the Chinese sexagenary cycle (the 60-year calendar system).
  • 善渊 - The name of the person who discovered the brick, likely a Daoist or religious figure.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 待榜次 - Literally 'waiting for the list of results.' Refers to waiting for the official examination results to be posted, a common practice in the imperial examination system.
  • 掷骰钱赌之 - To cast dice with coins to bet on it. This is a gambling or divination practice, where one uses dice (possibly made of coins) to determine an outcome.
  • 祝骰钱以卜前程 - To invoke the dice with coins to divine one’s future. This is a form of divination, where one makes a wish or prayer before casting the dice to seek omens or guidance.
  • 一掷得鱼 - One throw won the fish. A literal expression, but likely metaphorical or symbolic, as the fish is not a prize in the traditional sense.
  • 拊膺曰 - Clutched one’s chest and said. A gesture expressing deep emotion, often sorrow or despair.
  • 我家数口，绝食已二日 - My family of several people has gone without food for two days. A colloquial expression indicating hardship and desperation.
  • 就一熟分人赊此鱼 - I borrowed this fish from someone, having cooked one portion to share. Indicates the fish was obtained on credit or in exchange.
  • 望获数钱，以为举家之食 - Hoping to gain a few coins to feed the whole family. Shows the fish was a means of earning money to survive.
  • 子乃一掷胜之 - You won it with one throw. Suggests the fish was 'stolen' or taken unfairly.
  • 我家食禄尽矣 - Our family’s share of sustenance is over. A metaphorical expression meaning the family has no more food or means to survive.
  • 恻然哀之 - Moved with compassion and sorrow. A standard expression of sympathy.
  • 不取鱼，又以数钱遗之 - Did not take the fish, but gave him a few coins instead. Indicates a generous and empathetic response.
  • 谢而去 - Expressed gratitude and departed. A common formulaic expression.
  • 下第西归 - Failed the examination and returned westward. '下第' refers to failing the imperial examination.
  • 猝为群盗所执 - Suddenly seized by a group of bandits. Indicates a sudden and violent capture.
  • 天明阅客行囊 - At dawn, the bandits searched the travelers’ belongings. A standard bandit tactic.
  • 非京师邸中乞我鱼不取者乎 - Are you not the one who refused to take the fish I begged from you in the capital? A rhetorical question used to identify the person.
  • 再三慰谢 - Repeatedly consoled and thanked him. Indicates a gesture of gratitude and relief.
  • 并同行皆免 - And all those traveling with him were spared. Suggests that the bandits let everyone go after the identification.
  • 同郡徐安上记其事云 - The same county’s Xu Anshang recorded this event. A standard closing formula indicating the source of the story.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冯翊士人 - A scholar from Fengyi. Fengyi (冯翊) was an ancient Chinese commandery in the western part of the Han dynasty, located in present-day Shaanxi province. It was a region known for producing scholars and officials.
  • 元礼中试京师 - Passed the imperial examination in the capital during the Yuanli period. Yuanli (元礼) likely refers to the reign title of a Han dynasty emperor or a specific time period, though it is unclear. '中试' means passing the imperial examination.
  • 京师 - The capital. Refers to the imperial capital, likely Chang’an during the Han dynasty.
  • 骰钱 - Dice made of coins. A form of gambling or divination, common in ancient China.
  • 下第 - Failing the examination. A common term for those who did not pass the imperial exams.
  • 西归 - Returning westward. Indicates the direction of travel, likely back to the person’s home region.
  • 渑池 - Mianchi. A historical location in Henan province, known for its strategic importance and as a site of historical events.
  • 群盗 - Bandits. Refers to organized robbers or outlaws, common in ancient China.
  • 同郡徐安上 - Xu Anshang, a fellow countyman. Indicates that the story is recorded by a local historian or scholar from the same region as the subject.
